refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity output of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational data.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials over agreed duration (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage hauled), elapsed time, and safety incidents. Compare achieved productivity against target. Require Site Manager sign-off on representativeness and Safety Manager clearance on compliance.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, haul, dump) with controllable precision and repeatability.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall provide operator (on-site or remote) with sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to direct vehicle tasks.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety policies and prevent unsafe task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The solution shall measure and report task productivity metrics (volume, tonnage, time) in real-time and post-operation.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-precision hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with real-time telemetry and remote control capability, coupled with autonomous task planning and safety interlocks.
DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle platform with operator-in-loop control (on-site or tele-operated), instrumented with productivity sensors, and integrated with a safety policy enforcement layer.

DP-C: Implement a hybrid approach: vehicle with dual control modes (manual on-site, tele-operated remote), real-time telemetry streaming, safety alert system, and post-operation productivity analytics.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Implement a hybrid approach: vehicle with dual control modes (manual on-site, tele-operated remote), real-time telemetry streaming, safety alert system, and post-operation productivity analytics.

potential_sources: Teleoperation latency may couple with vehicle control precision; mitigated by local safety interlocks and operator training.
Productivity measurement depends on accurate sensor calibration; decoupled via independent sensor validation and post-operation audit.
Safety policy enforcement couples with task execution; managed via explicit safety gate before task initiation.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C avoids over-specification (DP-A's autonomous planning) and under-specification (DP-B's lack of remote capability). Hybrid control mode allows on-site operation for baseline productivity and remote operation for extended reach/safety. Telemetry and safety alert are orthogonal to control mode.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Network link supports tele-operation latency requirements (<500 ms round-trip for safe control).
Assumption: Operator training and certification are in scope for customer.
Risk: Dual control modes may introduce mode-switching errors; mitigated by explicit mode-lock and operator confirmation.
Risk: Productivity measurement accuracy depends on sensor calibration; requires pre-trial validation.
Risk: Safety policy enforcement may conflict with productivity targets if policies are overly restrictive; requires stakeholder alignment.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify with Site Manager and Safety Manager: (1) acceptable latency for tele-operation, (2) safety policy specifics (e.g., geofencing, speed limits, operator certification), (3) productivity measurement methodology (e.g., GPS-based volume, load cell tonnage, time-motion study).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration boundary encompassing Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services as sub-modules. Responsible for end-to-end productivity delivery, operator interface (on-site and remote), safety compliance, and telemetry aggregation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time and post-operation productivity metrics (volume, tonnage, time, task status) streamed to Site Manager and analytics backend.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Task directives from Site Manager or Tele-operator (e.g., dig location, haul destination, load target) received by vehicle control system.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety alerts and policy violations (e.g., geofence breach, speed overage, operator certification lapse) sent to Safety Manager and on-site personnel.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication channel for tele-operation commands, telemetry streaming, and safety alerts between on-site vehicle and remote tele-operation center.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary. All sub-functions (vehicle control, teleoperation, safety services, telemetry) are logically contained. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross boundary. Ownership: System Engineer (overall integration) with delegation to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-module owners.

Vehicle module contains hydraulic actuation, local control logic, and sensor instrumentation. However, productivity measurement (telemetry aggregation) and safety policy enforcement (SafetyAlert) are partially diffused to Teleoperation and Safety Services. Vehicle is a sub-module of NGHE Solution, not the top-level boundary for this FR.

Teleoperation module handles remote operator interface and command transmission. Productivity measurement and safety enforcement are diffused to NGHE Solution and Safety Services. Teleoperation is a sub-module, not the top-level boundary.

Safety Services enforces policies and generates alerts. However, task execution (Vehicle), operator control (Teleoperation), and productivity measurement (NGHE Solution) are outside this module. Safety Services is a sub-module, not the top-level boundary.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: System Engineer (NGHE Solution integration lead), with sub-module ownership: Vehicle Engineer (Vehicle), Teleoperation Engineer (Teleoperation), Safety Engineer (Safety Services).
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because it integrates all sub-functions (vehicle, teleoperation, safety services) and owns the end-to-end productivity KPI. Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link are external interfaces that connect NGHE Solution to Site Manager, Tele-operator, Safety Manager, and network infrastructure.
containment_risks: Risk: Telemetry aggregation logic (productivity calculation) may be diffused across Vehicle (sensor data), Teleoperation (operator input), and Safety Services (policy compliance). Mitigation: Designate a single Telemetry Aggregation sub-module within NGHE Solution.

Risk: Network Link reliability may be outside NGHE Solution scope (owned by IT/Network team). Mitigation: Define Network Link SLA (latency, availability) as a constraint and allocate responsibility to IT.

Risk: Safety policy enforcement may conflict with productivity targets if policies are not co-designed with Site Manager and Safety Manager. Mitigation: Conduct joint requirements workshop before detailed design.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm with System Engineer and Project Manager: (1) NGHE Solution is the correct top-level IM boundary for this FR, (2) sub-module ownership and interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are clearly defined, (3) Telemetry Aggregation logic is centralized to avoid diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid control, telemetry, safety alerts) directly addresses FR-NGHE-001 (productivity KPI). The DP enables on-site and remote operation, real-time measurement, and safety compliance. No logical gaps or contradictions. Coupling is acceptable with documented mitigations.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry aggregation logic may be diffused across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services if not explicitly owned by a single sub-module.
Network Link ownership and SLA may be unclear if IT/Network team is not engaged.
Safety policy enforcement may be diffused between Safety Services and Vehicle control if not clearly separated.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary. All sub-functions (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are clearly defined. Ownership is allocated to System Engineer with sub-module delegation. Recommended: Create detailed interface specifications and sub-module ownership matrix before detailed design.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via representative operations trials. Productivity metrics (volume, tonnage, time) can be measured using vehicle sensors and post-operation analytics. Safety compliance can be audited against worksite policies. Operator feedback can be collected via surveys and interviews.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution IM boundary. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry Aggregation sub-modules respectively. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Conduct joint requirements workshop with Site Manager, Safety Manager, and Tele-operator to finalize productivity target, safety policies, and operator certification requirements.
Define Network Link SLA (latency, availability, bandwidth) and allocate responsibility to IT/Network team.
Create detailed interface specifications for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link.
Establish sub-module ownership matrix and interface contracts for Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry Aggregation.
Plan representative operations trials with clear success criteria and measurement methodology.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that requires decomposition into sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) to enable detailed design and verification. The refined FR and selected DP are sound, and the IM assignment to NGHE Solution is correct. However, the sub-FRs must be further decomposed to allocate requirements to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry Aggregation sub-modules. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to achieve atomic, verifiable requirements.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, haul, dump) with controllable precision and repeatability.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall provide operator (on-site or remote) with sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to direct vehicle tasks.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety policies and prevent unsafe task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The solution shall measure and report task productivity metrics (volume, tonnage, time) in real-time and post-operation.

assumptions: Network link supports tele-operation latency requirements (<500 ms round-trip for safe control).
Operator training and certification are in scope for customer.
Productivity measurement methodology (GPS-based volume, load cell tonnage, time-motion study) is agreed with Site Manager.
Safety policies (geofencing, speed limits, operator certification) are defined by Safety Manager.
Representative operations trials are feasible and representative of customer's typical worksite conditions.
open_risks: Dual control modes may introduce mode-switching errors; requires explicit mode-lock and operator confirmation.
Productivity measurement accuracy depends on sensor calibration; requires pre-trial validation.
Safety policy enforcement may conflict with productivity targets if policies are overly restrictive; requires stakeholder alignment.
Network Link reliability and latency may be outside NGHE Solution scope; requires IT/Network team engagement.
Telemetry aggregation logic may be diffused across sub-modules if not explicitly owned.
evidence_needed: Network Link SLA specification (latency, availability, bandwidth) from IT/Network team.
Safety policy specification (geofencing, speed limits, operator certification) from Safety Manager.
Productivity measurement methodology and target values from Site Manager.
Operator training and certification requirements from Tele-operator and Equipment Operator.
Representative operations trial plan and success criteria from Project Manager.
Sensor calibration and validation procedures from Maintenance/Quality team.
